containers.description: Enhance your Workers with serverless containers.
containers.upgrade.title: Enable Containers
containers.upgrade.description: Containers is included in the Workers Paid plan. To start using Containers, upgrade your Workers plan.
containers.get_started.title: Get started with Containers
containers.get_started.description: With Containers, you can build applications that seamlessly integrate Workers with a serverless container runtime.
containers.get_started.steps.title: Deploy your first Container
containers.sidebar.cli.title: Start with CLI
containers.sidebar.cli.description: Get started with CLI for Workers for Platforms
containers.sidebar.analytics.requests.to: Total request count across all Workers in a dispatch namespace.
containers.sidebar.analytics.cpu_time.to: CPU time combined across Dispatch Workers and Workers in a dispatch  namespace.
containers.sidebar.analytics.scripts.too: The number of Workers combined across all dispatch namespaces.
containers.connect.title: Connect to your container with SSH
containers.connect.disabled.title: Enable SSH to connect to this instance
containers.connect.disabled.description: Enable ssh in your Wrangler config, then redeploy your Worker before connecting with SSH.
containers.connect.not_running.title: The container must be running to connect via SSH
containers.connect.unsupported.title: This instance does not support SSH
containers.connect.authorized_keys: Connecting via SSH requires configuring authorized_keys in wrangler.toml or wrangler.jsonc.
containers.connect.docs_link: Learn more in the SSH docs
containers.metrics.aggregate.tooltip: Toggle between metrics for each instance and aggregate metrics across all instances
containers.metrics.aggregate.disabled.to: Metrics must be aggregated when there are over %{limit} instances
containers.container.no_instances: This container has no instances. If you have just deployed the container, it may take a few minutes for the instances to be provisioned
containers.container.no_instances.ready: You have no running containers (but they are ready to go)
containers.container.no_instances.check_: If you just created this application, instances may still be provisioning.
containers.container.no_instances.title: No active instances
containers.tutorials.title: Tutorials
containers.tutorials.description: Learn how to use Containers with step-by-step tutorials.
containers.tutorials.description_empty: Learn how to build and deploy your first Container with step-by-step tutorials.
containers.usage.title: Usage
containers.container.logs_disabled.desct: To enable Container logs, add the snippet below to your Worker's <0>wrangler.jsonc</0> config:
Then redeploy your Worker with <0>wrangler deploy</0>.
containers.deployments.empty.title: No deployments
containers.deployments.empty.description: This account does not have any raw deployments.
containers.deployments.filters.title: Filters
containers.deployments.filters.label: Label
containers.deployments.filters.labels.in: Enter both a name and value for each label.
Label names cannot contain a colon.
containers.deployments.logs_disabled.des: Logging cannot be enabled for an existing raw deployment. Create a new deployment with logging enabled to collect logs.
containers.deployments.overview.no_place: This deployment does not have a current placement.
containers.deployments.events.empty.desc: This deployment does not have any recorded events.
containers.deployments.events.unsupporte: Event history is not available for deployments created before this feature was enabled.
containers.state.updating.info: A new version of this container is being rolled out.
containers.state.degraded.info: This container has launched but one or more instances have errored or failed. It may recover automatically.
containers.state.active.info: This container is up and has a live instance running.
containers.state.ready.info: This container is ready but no instances are active.
containers.state.provisioning.info: This container is currently being provisioned. It may be able to serve some traffic.
containers.state.no_instances.info: This container has no instances configured.
containers.state.unknown.info: The status of this container is unknown.
containers.rollout.info: Live instances are being updated to a new Container configuration.
containers.versions.description: Only active versions are shown. Versions with 0% of traffic will be pruned.
containers.versions.error: Failed to load versions. Please refresh the page to try again.
containers.errors.container_exit.message: The container encountered an error at runtime and exited. Your logs might contain more information.
containers.errors.usage_analytics_query: Failed to load usage analytics. Please refresh the page to try again.
containers.delete_modal.title: Delete this Container?
containers.delete_modal.description: Are you sure you want to delete the Container: "%{containerAppName}"? All instances will be removed and any Workers using this Container may fail. This action is permanent and cannot be undone.
